leads.name: Canarium Books
leads.title: Editor and Co-founder
leads.notes: Poetry press based in Ann Arbor, Michigan -- the same city as Cushing-Malloy. Founded 2008 by Joshua Edwards, Robyn Schiff, Nick Twemlow, and Lynn Xu. Published 33 collections distributed by Asterism Books. Proximity and shared literary community make this a natural manufacturing partner.

leads.name: Glass Lyre Press
leads.title: Publisher and Managing Editor
leads.notes: Independent literary press in Glenview, IL, just north of Chicago -- about 4 hours from Ann Arbor. Ami Kaye publishes poetry collections, chapbooks, fiction, and anthologies, including the Aeolian Harp Anthology Folio Series and Pirene's Fountain Poetry Journal. Active multilingual and multicultural publishing mission with a catalog of 50 or more titles.

leads.name: Dos Madres Press
leads.title: Executive Editor
leads.notes: Loveland, OH near Cincinnati -- within 5 hours of Ann Arbor. Executive editor Robert J. Murphy has published hundreds of poetry titles since the press's founding. Known for commitment to literary poetry outside the MFA-contest circuit. Currently pausing new submissions due to backlog, signaling a productive and active press.

leads.name: Tiger Bark Press
leads.title: Publisher
leads.notes: Rochester, NY. Founded 2007 by poet Steven Huff, now published by Philip Memmer (poet and Hamilton College faculty). Planning a December 2025 open reading period. Focuses on innovative and diverse contemporary poetry voices. Active literary community presence in upstate New York.

leads.name: Terrapin Books
leads.title: Founder, Publisher, and Editor
leads.notes: West Caldwell, NJ. Founded 2016 by poet Diane Lockward (Poet Laureate of West Caldwell NJ). Active spring 2026 releases include Geraldine Connolly, Michael Hettich, Kiyoko Reidy, and Clare Banks. Strong critical reception and award history. Publishes 6-8 poetry titles per year with excellent production standards.

leads.name: Trio House Press
leads.title: Executive Director
leads.notes: Minneapolis, MN. Executive director Kris Bigalk leading major 2026 expansion -- 10 or more poetry books plus acquiring Red Dragonfly Press for a new eco-poetry imprint. Active award programs: Louise Bogan Award and new Aurora Polaris Award. Published debut The Grace of Black Mothers (July 2025) and memoir Lost Found Kept (early 2025).

leads.name: Orison Books
leads.title: Founder and Editor
leads.notes: Asheville, NC. Nonprofit literary press founded and edited by Luke Hankins, focused on the life of the spirit in contemporary poetry and fiction. Active 2026 Orison Prizes judged by Leila Chatti (poetry) and Margot Livesey (fiction). Also publishes Asheville Poetry Review. Strong editorial mission and award-winning catalog.

leads.name: Black Ocean
leads.title: Founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Boston, MA with staff also in Chicago and Detroit. Founded 2004 by Janaka Stucky. Award-winning press known for visually striking book design and visionary poetry. Offering 2026 annual subscription of 8 titles. Staff presence in Detroit is particularly notable for proximity to Cushing-Malloy in Ann Arbor.

leads.name: Rose Metal Press
leads.title: Co-founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Brookline, MA. Founded 2006 by Abigail Beckel and Kathleen Rooney (author of 11 books). Pioneer in hybrid and cross-genre literary publishing: flash fiction, prose poetry, lyric essays, novellas-in-flash. Field guides and anthologies alongside single-author collections. Unique niche with strong editorial identity.

leads.name: Sibling Rivalry Press
leads.title: Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Little Rock, AR. LGBTQ+ focused literary press founded 2010 by Bryan Borland and Seth Pennington. Re-launched Assaracus journal in 2025 with expanded scope to include all LGBTQIA+ identified poets. Active and growing catalog. Seth Pennington is editor-in-chief managing day-to-day operations.

leads.name: Diode Editions
leads.title: Founder and Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Richmond, VA. Founded by Patty Paine (professor at VCUarts Qatar) as an outgrowth of Diode Poetry Journal. 80 published titles of poetry, chapbooks, and poetry-related nonfiction. Law Alsobrook serves as editor and art director. Annual book and chapbook contests. Established track record with strong design aesthetic.

leads.name: BrickHouse Books
leads.title: President, Publisher, and Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: Baltimore, MD. Founded 1970 -- oldest continuously operating independent literary press in Maryland. Clarinda Harriss is the president, publisher, and editor-in-chief with decades of institutional knowledge. Currently open year-round for prose, poetry, and nonfiction submissions. Long track record of quality literary publishing in the Mid-Atlantic region.

leads.name: Lavender Ink / Dialogos
leads.title: Founder, Director, and Editor-in-Chief
leads.notes: New Orleans, LA. Founded 1995 by Bill Lavender. Two imprints: Lavender Ink (poetry, fiction, literary criticism) and Dialogos (works in translation and cross-cultural literature). Distributed by Asterism Books. Active 8-12 titles/year. Long history of supporting experimental and innovative literary work from a New Orleans base.

leads.name: Fonograf Editions
leads.title: Founder, Executive Director, and Co-Publisher
leads.notes: Portland, OR. 501(c)(3) nonprofit press and literary record label founded 2016 by Jeff Alessandrelli and Adie Steckel. Distributed by NYU Press. Unique model combining poetry books with music recordings. Known for innovative design and production. Active 2025-2026 catalog including Andrew Colarusso's Pettygod (2026) and Jay Wright's Parodos (2026).

leads.name: Tavern Books
leads.title: Managing Editor
leads.notes: Portland, OR. Nonprofit poetry press led by founding editor Carl Adamshick and managing editor Natalie Garyet. Specializes in original poetry collections, works in translation (Living Library series), and select reprints. Established track record with a clear translation and reprint mission alongside original titles.

leads.name: Perugia Press
leads.title: Director and Editor
leads.notes: Florence, MA. Nonprofit feminist press founded 1997, publishing one first/second collection by a woman annually via the Perugia Press Prize. Rebecca Olander is director and editor (Westfield State University professor). 29 collections published. Small output but high editorial standards and strong reputation in feminist poetry.

leads.name: Brick Road Poetry Press
leads.title: Publisher and Co-owner
leads.notes: Acquired summer 2024 by Matthew Layne, Miriam Calleja, Halley Cotton, Barry Marks. Moved to Birmingham, AL. New ownership team of poets and literary professionals bringing fresh vision. Active 2025-26 book contest. Contact email is generic submissions address; direct email for Matthew Layne not located after thorough research.

leads.name: Subito Press
leads.title: Managing Editor
leads.notes: University of Colorado Boulder. Student-run press with faculty oversight by Elisabeth Sheffield. Accepts fiction, poetry, and hybrid work. Submissions active October 2025 through January 2026. Rachel Franklin Wood is managing editor. University literary press focusing on experimental and innovative work.

leads.name: Kore Press
leads.title: Publisher and Executive Director
leads.notes: Tucson, AZ. Founded 1993. Feminist literary press publishing poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction by women -- cis, trans, and gender-nonconforming. Lisa Bowden has led the press for decades as publisher and executive director. 30-plus years of institutional history. 2-3 contests per year plus January open submissions.

leads.name: Seven Kitchens Press
leads.title: Founder and Publisher
leads.notes: Lewisburg, PA. Founded 2007 by poet Ron Mohring. Published 200-plus chapbooks through 8 series: Keystone (PA poets), Robin Becker (LGBTQIA+), ReBound (reprints), Editor's Series, Rane Arroyo Series, Summer Kitchen, A.V. Christie (women over 50), Allison Joseph (BIPOC women writers). Strong community mission. Contact email is general press address; Ron Mohring is sole founder and publisher.
